parsedContent.feedback: The answer correctly identifies three parts found only in plant cells and accurately describes their functions. The description of the cell wall, chloroplasts, and large central vacuole aligns with the model answer, demonstrating a clear understanding of the question.
parsedContent.correctedAnswer: 1. Cell wall (cellulose) — provides structural support and shape to the cell. 2. Chloroplasts — contain chlorophyll and are the site of photosynthesis. 3. Large central vacuole — stores water and maintains cell turgor pressure (rigidity).
